name: A. A. Milne
description: British author known for creating Winnie-the-Pooh (1882–1956)
abstract: Alan Alexander  Milne ( ; 18 January 1882 – 31 January 1956) was an English writer best known for his books about the teddy bear Winnie-the-Pooh, as well as children's poetry. Milne was primarily a playwright before the huge success of Winnie-the-Pooh overshadowed his previous work.

works_created.t: Wurzel-Flummery, A Comedy in One Act

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: I used it to produce “The Children’s Suite” which is a piece of music based on A. A. Milne’s poems.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
